item.title: BUP hosts int’l conference on South Asian security
item.description: The two-day ‘1st CFS International Conference’ concluded at the faculty of security and strategic studies of Bangladesh University of Professionals on Wednesday, focusing on national security issues in South Asia amid the changing global order......Politics

item.title: 26pc female office-goers commute by non-AC bus in city
item.description: Around 26 per cent of women office goers commute by non-air-conditioned buses in the capital, according to a recent survey of the Dhaka Transport Coordination Authority......

item.title: July martyrs recalled thru folk songs
item.description: The July Shaheed Smrity Foundation held a folk song event to remember the martyrs of the student-led uprising of July-August in 2024 at the Shaheed Mir Mugdho Mancha of Uttara in Dhaka on Wednesday......

item.title: ‘Women in Art’ a panorama of creative imagination
item.description: A group art exhibition titled ‘Women in Art’ concluded on Wednesday at Abinta Gallery of Fine Arts in the capital made a foray into diverse mediums, exploring the many shades of women’s life and womanhood......

item.title: 3 Bangladeshi films screened at London film festival
item.description: Three Bangladeshi films were screened at the 27th Rainbow International Film Festival in London in the United Kingdom......Expat Community Forum

item.title: SKOP leaders demand special allocation for labourers in upcoming budget
item.description: Leaders of the Sramik Karmachari Oikya Parishad at a rally in Dhaka on Wednesday called on the government to keep special allocation in the upcoming budget for the welfares of the labourers......

item.title: 4 workers injured in N’ganj boiler explosion
item.description: Four workers were injured in an explosion at a steel mill in Rasulpur area of Fatullah in Narayanganj on Wednesday......

item.title: IUB holds Career and Networking Day 2026
item.description: Independent University, Bangladesh organised its annual Career and Networking Day in Dhaka on Wednesday, drawing participation from over 60 organisations spanning a wide range of industries......

item.title: Kangaroo Care model can crucially reduce neonatal death: BMU VC
item.description: Bangladesh Medical University vice-chancellor Professor Dr FM Siddiqui at a seminar in Dhaka on Wednesday said that the Kangaroo Mother Care model was spreading light of hope in reducing mortality, preventing diseases and saving lives of new-borns, including premature or immature and low-weight new-borns......City & Local Guides

item.title: British Council calls for adopting language-responsive edn systems
item.description: The British Council has launched a global position paper urging countries to adopt language-responsive education systems that reflect learners’ linguistic realities and promote inclusive and effective education......

item.title: Cattle smuggling from India before Eid frustrates farmers in Sylhet
item.description: Rampant smuggling of cattle from India into the country through illegal border crossings along the Sylhet division has allegedly been going on for several weeks before Eid-ul-Azha, one of the largest religious festival of Muslims, worrying local farmers about getting fair prices for their domestic animals......

item.title: Clash over VGF card distribution turns violent in Kushtia
item.description: A violent clash broke out at Fulbaria Union Parishad of Kushtia on Tuesday over the distribution of Vulnerable Group Feeding cards, with allegations of armed attack, vandalism and assault on officials......

item.title: Fresh bomb scare grips Meherpur
item.description: Panic and uncertainty continue to spread in Meherpur after police recovered another bomb-like object and a handwritten note from Gangni upazila early Wednesday, the latest in a series of similar incidents reported in the district this month......

item.title: Ban unfit motor vehicles for safe Eid travel: discussion
item.description: The Health Sector of Dhaka Ahsania Mission held a discussion titled The Mass Media Role in Road Safety at the meeting room of the mission at Shyamoli in Dhaka on Wednesday......

item.title: UIU hosts youth symposium on environment, sustainability
item.description: The department of environment and development studies of United International University and the Environment, Development and Sustainability Club jointly organised the ‘Youth Symposium on Environment, Development & Sustainability’ on the campus in Dhaka on Tuesday......Press Release Distribution

item.title: DUET students enforce ‘complete shutdown’ over VC appointment
item.description: A section of students of Dhaka University of Engineering and Technology enforced a ‘complete shutdown’ programme on Wednesday, protesting the appointment of a new vice-chancellor which brought academic and administrative activities on the campus to a standstill......

item.title: Hormuz blockade could trigger global food crisis: UN
item.description: The UN Food and Agriculture Organisation warned Wednesday that the blockage of the Strait of Hormuz could ‘trigger a severe global food price crisis’ in the coming months......

item.title: Iran threatens wider war if US resumes attacks
item.description: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards warned on Wednesday that the Middle East war will extend beyond the region if the United States and Israel resume attacks on the Islamic republic......

item.title: Xi, Putin signal united front against US in Beijing talks
item.description: Chinese president Xi Jinping and visiting Russian president Vladimir Putin have signalled a united front against Washington during a summit in Beijing, warning against a global return to the ‘law of the jungle’, reports Al Jazeera......

item.title: Bangladesh clean sweep Pakistan
item.description: Taijul Islam’s six-for led Bangladesh to a memorable 78-run victory over Pakistan in the second and final Test to clinch the series 2-0 at the Sylhet International Cricket Stadium on Wednesday......Expat Community Forum

item.title: PDB faces strong opposition to proposed 21pc bulk price hike
item.description: The Bangladesh Power Development Board on Wednesday proposed a 21 per cent hike in wholesale electricity prices amid strong opposition from consumer rights groups, business leaders andpoliticalactivists......

item.title: BSF pushes in 9 Bangladeshis through Sylhet border
item.description: Border Security Force of India pushed nine people, including children and women, into Bangladesh through a border point at Goainghat upazila in Sylhet late on Tuesday night......

item.title: Fakhrul slates Jamaat over 1971 role, Jamaat denies allegations
item.description: Bangladesh Nationalist Party secretary general Mirza Fakhrul Islam Alamgir on Wednesday said that the people of Bangladesh would never bring the Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami to power for opposing the War of Independence in 1971 and weaponising of religion in politics......

item.title: Revenue falls Tk 1.04 lakh crore short of target
item.description: The country’s revenue collection faced a shortfall of Tk 1,04,533 crore in July–April of the current 2025–26 financial year, according to National Board of Revenue data......

item.title: Govt faces backlash over SC secretariat abolition
item.description: The Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government on Wednesday came under mounting criticism from lawyers and major political parties for attaching officials of the Supreme Court Secretariat to the law ministry after abolishing the secretariat......Politics

item.title: Govt’s worrying retreat on judicial independence
item.description: THE BNP government’s decision to abolish the Supreme Court secretariat marks a worrying retreat from one of its most important democratic commitments. The party has for years spoken forcefully, also in its 31-point reform agenda and election manifesto, about the need for the independence of the judiciary after what it described as the systematic misuse of courts and......City & Local Guides

item.title: Responsible business standards must for trade survival
item.description: Businesses need to improve their approach to the global responsible business conduct agenda for a competitive edge in international trade against regional competitors and to attract investors. The responsible business conduct agenda refers to a set of principles, policies and practices that encourage companies to operate in a way that is ethical, transparent and.....

item.title: A nation in three tongues
item.description: I REMEMBER Beirut in the years after its wars, when the city still carried the smell of cordite beneath the perfume of its cafés. The walls were patched, the militias renamed, the checkpoints gone — or hidden behind ministries and men in suits — but the divisions remained embedded like old shrapnel in the masonry of everyday life. I once asked both Sunni......

item.title: Inadequacy of tortious liability
item.description: THERE has been a rapid growth in construction because of urbanisation and real estate development. The lack of duty of care, negligence and weak supervision by Rajdhani Unnayan Kartripakkha have, however, led to accidents such as building collapses and structural failure. Unfortunately, there is no codified statute that establishes and comprehensively governs the......

item.title: Invisible workers of tea garden
item.description: BANGLADESH produces over 90 million kilograms of tea a year. The workers who make that possible earn Tk 187 a day......

item.title: Trump and Xi establish a vital floor
item.description: THE international economic order has entered a period of profound structural transformation, compelling a reassessment of how the world’s two largest economies manage their deep systemic interdependencies. Over the past several years, the prevailing consensus across global capitals has been that the financial and industrial relationship between Washington and......

item.title: US charges Raul Castro, Cuba says no legal basis
item.description: The Justice Department charged the influential former president over the deadly 1996 downing of two civilian planes manned by anti-Castro pilots......

item.title: UNICEF says procurement process behind vaccine crisis
item.description: UNICEF on Wednesday said that the vaccine shortage in Bangladesh was created not because of a funding crisis, but due to delays and indecision surrounding changes in the vaccine procurement process introduced in 2024, and that it had repeatedly warned the government about a possible vaccine outage......Expat Community Forum

item.title: Couple sent to jail over killing 8-year-old girl at Mirpur
item.description: Dhaka courts on Wednesday sent the prime accused and his wife to jail in a case filed over the brutal rape and murder of an eight-year-old girl child whose beheaded body was recovered in the capital’s Mirpur area......

item.title: ICT charges ex-AL MP Hasnat Abdullah, 3 cops with extrajudicial killings
item.description: The International Crimes Tribunal-2 on Wednesday charged former Awami League lawmaker Abul Hasanat Abdullah, former Barishad superintendent of police AKM Ehsan Ullah and two other police members with crime against humanity for murders of two BNP activists in a reported crossfire at Agailjhara in 2015......

item.title: Ex-military dictator Ershad’s wife Bidisha jailed for two years
item.description: A Dhaka metropolitan magistrate court on Wednesday jailed former military dictator HM Ershad’s wife Bidisha Ershad for two years in a fraud case filed with the Gulshan police station in 2018......

item.title: Four workers die in septic tank at Sarail
item.description: Four workers died after entering a septic tank of an under-construction building at Sarail upazila of Brahmanbaria on Wednesday afternoon......City & Local Guides

item.title: China training Russian troops for Ukraine combat
item.description: European intelligence agencies believe the Chinese military has trained hundreds of Russian soldiers for fighting in Ukraine, according to Germany’s Die Welt newspaper which cited secret documents it had seen......

item.title: Ebola, hantavirus show world’s risk preparedness lagging: expert
item.description: The deadly hantavirus and Ebola outbreaks show that while the response to declared public health crises has improved, awareness of pandemic risks still lags, a leading pandemic expert warned Tuesday......

item.title: Russian strike kills two in Ukraine’s Dnipro
item.description: A Russian attack on Ukraine’s eastern Dnipro city killed two people and wounded six others, regional military authorities said on Wednesday......

item.title: 8 killed in road accidents
item.description: At least eight people were killed in road accidents in Bagerhat, Sunamganj, Thakurgaon and Tangail on Wednesday......

item.title: Four sentenced to death for army officer killing
item.description: A Cox’s Bazar court on Wednesday sentenced four people to death and nine others to life imprisonment for killing an army officer during a drive by joint force in Dulahazara area of Chakaria upazila in the district in 2024......

item.title: Chhatra Dal leader threatens RU hall provost over seat occupation
item.description: A leader of Bangladesh Jatiyatabadi Chhatra Dal, student wing of ruling Bangladesh Nationalist Party, allegedly threatened the provost of Nawab Abdul Latif Hall at Rajshahi University over the occupation of two seats at the hall......

item.title: Govt urged to increase WASH allocations in next budget
item.description: Water and sanitation advocacy groups on Wednesday urged the government to increase allocation in the coming national budget for the water, sanitation and hygiene to help ensuring equitable WASH services for all citizens......

item.title: No political identity of criminals will be considered: DMP commissioner
item.description: Newly appointed Dhaka Metropolitan Police commissioner Mosleh Uddin Ahmed on Wednesday vowed to follow a ‘zero tolerance’ policy against crime and extortion in the capital, asserting that no offender would receive consideration based on theirpoliticalor social identity......
